items: Option to show or hide YAML frontmatter in notes
Toggle setting for task indicators across the app (enabled by default)
Rename files directly from the tab bar by double-clicking a tab
Refined heading styling so H1 and H2 are more in line with editor heading visuals
Updated orange accent to a more pleasant hex tone for better visual comfort
Improved image handling for wiki-style images and image linking
Switched task progress from bars to rings for clearer progress at a glance
Added task progress rings in the sidebar
Compact tab bar layout for a cleaner, denser UI
Fixed and cleaned up issues with smaller font sizes
Tightened sidebar spacing and recent spaces button UI alignment
Print notes to PDF. Save any note as a PDF for sharing, archiving, or offline reading
Optional Vim keybindings. Power users can now move through notes, edit faster, and stay in flow with familiar Vim-style controls
Switch spaces instantly from the menubar or sidebar for faster navigation between your workspaces
Native llama.cpp support. Run blazing-fast local LLMs with zero configuration, perfect for privacy-first users who want AI on their own terms
Adjustable editor width. Choose Narrow for focused reading, Comfortable for balanced composition, or Wide to harness your full screen while writing
Command palette got a cleaner layout with smarter organization so you can find what you need faster
Appearance settings now live in one tidy Icon & Color submenu with colors up top and icons below
Pruned a bunch of old unused code so the app stays lean and runs smoother
Cleaned All Notes UI for better readability and easier scanning of your note list
Notes Cards inside All Notes UI now show preview with formatting and not just raw markdown
Notes Cards inside Kanban boards also now show preview with formatting and not just raw markdown
AI moved from a sidebar to a floating panel inside the editor, so you can chat and write in one focused workspace
Improved Readability for Tags and Pills with better contrast and clearer styling
Bug that wouldn't let some users switch AI providers in the settings is now fixed
Cleaner menus — formatting options only appear when you are editing Markdown, keeping the menubar simple the rest of the time.
Refined Calendar Activity into a clean, command-palette-style note list with title-plus-folder scanning for faster daily context
Copy button on code blocks — grab code snippets with a single click, with a satisfying checkmark to confirm
Improved Dark Mode accessibility with refined contrast and readability so every screen feels more comfortable and polished after hours
Glyph now keeps saving normally on SMB and NAS shares, and only skips the unsupported sync call when the filesystem reports that it cannot handle it
Streamlined calendar button styling for a more consistent and polished appearance across all controls
Settings UI refresh — cleaner folder paths and simpler descriptions throughout the Space settings
Local graph view for exploring directly connected notes from the current note
Optional colorful heading colors in Advanced settings for rich note editing
Improved and Simplified Onboarding
You can now choose exactly where note attachments live: in the space root, in one dedicated folder, or right beside each note
Glyph also keeps Git Sync smarter here, only treating attachments as a special tracked folder when you have chosen a dedicated attachment folder
Git Sync now runs in the background, so Glyph stays responsive while your changes are fetched, merged, and pushed
Refreshed Git Sync with a cleaner footer control that feels more at home in the app
Live Git Sync status now updates in real time, so it is easier to see progress and spot issues right away
Improved app stability and responsiveness by reducing UI state glitches across tabs, command palette, AI, databases, calendar, and file management
Smoother, more delightful animations throughout the app
Task progress indicator in editor ribbon showing completed/total tasks
Web link saving with automatic metadata extraction
People as tags option for contacts and mentions
Native macOS 26+ dynamic light/dark/clear app icons
Major codebase cleanup — eliminated 1,000+ lines of unused CSS and dead code for faster, leaner performance
Streamlined the updating flow to make it more user-friendly
File open performance — 30-40% faster on average
Today view design cleanup and polish
Settings panel now opens inline within the main window
Collections and databases UI refinements
Consolidated sidebar navigation — All Notes, Daily Notes, and Templates now live under a single item
LM Studio and local OpenAI-compatible endpoints now connect reliably
